title: torilis arvensis var. purpurea
intro: Torilis arvensis var. purpurea, also known as purple hedge-parsley, is a variety of the Torilis arvensis plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its dark purple flowers and feathery foliage. The medicinal properties of purple hedge-parsley include its ability to act as a diuretic, lower blood pressure, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of purple hedge-parsley include its potential to support kidney health, reduce the risk of kidney stones, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Purple hedge-parsley can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or it can be dried and used in capsule or tincture form to harness its medicinal properties.
